snp1.7:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
snp1.7:1.3:
Then, in the early morning, after adjusting his lower robe and carrying his bowl & outer robe,
he entered Sāvatthī for alms.
snp1.7:1.4:
Now at that time, in the house of the brahman Aggika Bhāradvāja,
a (sacrificial) fire was burning and an offering was lifted up.
snp1.7:1.5: Then the Blessed One, going through Sāvatthī on a methodical almsround, approached the house of Aggika Bhāradvāja.
snp1.7:2.1: Aggika Bhāradvāja saw the Blessed One coming from afar and, on seeing him, said to him:
snp1.7:3.1: When this was said, the Blessed One said to the brahman Aggika Bhāradvāja,
snp1.7:3.2: “But do you know, brahman, what an outcaste is, or the actions that make one an outcaste?”
snp1.7:3.3: “No, in fact, Master Gotama, I don’t know what an outcaste is, or the actions that make one an outcaste.
snp1.7:3.4:
It would be good if Master Gotama taught me the Dhamma so that I would
know what an outcaste is and the actions that make one an outcaste.”
snp1.7:3.5:
“In that case, brahman, listen and pay close attention.
I will speak.”
snp1.7:3.6: “As you say, master,” the brahman Aggika Bhāradvāja responded to the Blessed One.
snp1.7:4.3: deceitful, and defective in his views:
snp1.7:5.2: Whoever here harms a living being once-born or twice-born,
snp1.7:5.3: who has no sympathy for a living being:
snp1.7:7.3: takes in a manner of theft what others claim as ‘mine’:
snp1.7:9.2: strikes a person going along a road,
snp1.7:10.2: Whatever man, for his own sake, the sake of another, or the sake of wealth,
snp1.7:10.3: tells a lie when asked to bear witness:
snp1.7:11.2: Whoever misbehaves with the wives of relatives or friends,
snp1.7:11.3: by force or with their consent:
snp1.7:12.3: Whoever, though capable, doesn’t support his mother or father —old, their youth over & done:
snp1.7:13.3: Whoever strikes and reviles with his speech mother or father, sister or brother, or mother-in-law:
snp1.7:14.3: and gives counsel concealing some points:
snp1.7:16.1: Whoever, having gone to another’s house,
snp1.7:16.3: doesn’t honor (the host) in return when he comes (to one’s house):
snp1.7:17.3: Whoever deceives with a lie a brahman, contemplative, or other mendicant:
snp1.7:18.1: Whoever, when a brahman or contemplative
snp1.7:18.3: reviles him with speech and doesn’t give:
snp1.7:19.1: Whoever, wrapped in delusion, speaks here what is untrue,
snp1.7:21.1: Angry, mean, evil in his desires,
snp1.7:22.1: Whoever heaps verbal abuse on an Awakened One
snp1.7:23.3: He is the thief in this world with its Brahmās.
snp1.7:23.4: He is the vilest of outcastes.
snp1.7:23.5: These are said to be outcastes,
snp1.7:23.6: as I have proclaimed them to you.
snp1.7:24.1: Not by birth is one an outcaste,
snp1.7:24.3: By action one is an outcaste.
snp1.7:24.4: By action is one a brahman.
snp1.7:25.3: Sopāka, the son of an outcaste,
snp1.7:26.1: He, Mātaṅga, attained the highest prestige,
snp1.7:27.4: he reached the world of the Brahmās.
snp1.7:27.6: from reaching the world of the Brahmās.
snp1.7:28.1: Though born into a family of scholars,
snp1.7:28.2: brahmans, with chants as their kinsmen,
snp1.7:28.4: are repeatedly seen with evil deeds:
snp1.7:29.2: with a bad destination in the afterlife.
snp1.7:30.1: Not by birth is one an outcaste,
snp1.7:30.3: By action one is an outcaste.
snp1.7:30.4: By action is one a brahman.
snp1.7:31.1: When this was said, the brahman Aggika Bhāradvāja said to the Blessed One:
snp1.7:31.2:
“Magnificent, Master Gotama!
Magnificent!
Just as if he were to place upright what was overturned, to reveal what was hidden, to show the way to one who was lost,
or to carry a lamp into the dark so that those with eyes could see forms,
in the same way has Master Gotama—through many lines of reasoning—made the Dhamma clear.
snp1.7:31.3:
I go to Master Gotama for refuge, to the Dhamma, & to the Saṅgha of monks.
May Master Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge from this day forward, for life.”
